series_metadata.series_description: Under section 4503.10 of the Ohio Revised Code, any owner of a motor vehicle that operates on the highways shall file each year at a Deputy Registrars office or the Bureau of Motor Vehicles an application for vehicle registration. The application is an all purpose form used for issuing passenger, non-commercial, motor home, motorcycle, house vehicle, commercial truck, commercial trailer, farm truck, bus, transfer, temporary tag registrations, etc.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Paper: Effective January 1, 2004, all applications generated this date are be retained 2 and 1/ from date of issue ; Electronic: Vehicle Registration Database System (LEADS) - an individual renews his/her vehicle registration. current information will overlay existing record. Transaction Database System - Effective November 1996, all information from vehicle registrations issued by Deputy Registrars will be stored in this system 3 years from date of generation Print On Demand Electronic Files - Effective January 1, 2004, all electronic registration files generated this date are be retained 2 and 1/ from date of issue, archived. ; Microform: microfilm is duplicated, and original film is stored in unit. duplicate microfilm is kept in off-site storage located at Shipley Bldg. Registration applications issued prior implementation of Print On Demand (POD) shall be retained seven years from date of issue
retention_rules.disposition_method: Paper: Recycle ; Electronic: Delete ; Microform: Shred
